US25 – Time Delta Plot

Three UI concepts for baseline selection and delta visualisation. Click interactive elements to see state changes.

Concept A

Roster radio button → "Δ delta" Y-metric button

Baseline is chosen in the roster; delta mode is a new first-class Y option beside cumul./rolling/etc.

Roster

3 / 10

★ = set as baseline

Y:
X:
cumulative average pace
Pace (min/km)
Time delta (s)

Select a baseline run in the roster (★)

Pros

  • ✓ Baseline is glanceable in roster
  • ✓ Delta is a first-class Y mode — familiar pattern
  • ✓ Other Y metrics still work while baseline set

Cons

  • ✗ Roster panel needs new radio/star controls
  • ✗ Delta Y-seg button is visually odd (different meaning to others)
  • ✗ Two separate places to configure one feature

Interactions

  • • Click ★ on roster item → sets as baseline
  • • Click "Δ delta" Y button → switches plot to delta
  • • If delta selected with no baseline: show prompt
Concept B

Baseline dropdown in plot controls → delta auto-activates

A "Baseline:" select in the controls bar. Picking one auto-switches Y to delta. Roster unchanged.

Roster

3 / 10

  • London Marathon 2024

    42.2 km · 3:58/km

  • London Marathon 2023

    42.2 km · 4:06/km

  • Manchester Marathon 2024

    42.4 km · 4:01/km

Y:
X:
Baseline:
cumulative average pace
Pace (min/km)
Time delta (s)

Pros

  • ✓ Everything in one bar — self-contained
  • ✓ Roster panel needs zero changes
  • ✓ Dropdown makes selection/deselection obvious

Cons

  • ✗ Controls bar becomes more crowded
  • ✗ Auto-switching Y to delta may surprise users
  • ✗ Long activity names overflow the dropdown

Interactions

  • • Select baseline → plot switches to delta + Y label updates
  • • Select "— none —" → reverts to normal Y metrics
  • • Only roster members appear in dropdown
Concept C

Roster click-to-baseline + "Δ" overlay toggle in plot header

Clicking a roster item sets it as baseline (replaces current select-to-focus). A Δ toggle in the plot header switches view. Delta overlays the normal series.

Roster

3 / 10

Click to set baseline

Y:
X:
vs.
Pace (min/km)
Time delta (s)

Click a run in the roster to set it as the baseline, then enable Δ

Pros

  • ✓ Roster click repurposed cleanly (was: select to focus)
  • ✓ Δ toggle is minimal — one button on the right
  • ✓ Baseline name shown inline → clear context

Cons

  • ✗ Repurposing roster click may confuse existing users
  • ✗ No Y-metric flexibility in delta mode
  • ✗ Δ toggle without baseline selected needs graceful handling

Interactions

  • • Click roster item → highlighted as baseline
  • • Click "Δ Time delta" → switches chart; shows baseline name
  • • Toggle off or click same roster item → reverts

Comparison summary

Criterion A B C
Roster panel changes New radio/star per item None Repurpose click action
Plot controls changes One new Y-seg button New dropdown (crowded bar) One new toggle button
Baseline discoverability Good — in roster Good — in controls Medium — click-to-set pattern not obvious
Scope of code change Medium Small Medium + behaviour break
Story AC coverage Full Full Full (if Y label updated)
Recommendation Good default choice Lowest risk, smallest diff Cleanest UX but most disruptive
